Label.title: Novel hypothesis not previously in the clinician's repertoire
Backward reasoning from symptoms to underlying pathomechanism
Hypothesis triggered by direct sensory perception or epistemic action
If hypothesis H is true, finding F should be present; test F
Applying a codified clinical guideline or protocol mechanically
Retrospective coherence check of working diagnosis against all data
Rapid illness-script matching from a symptom constellation
Pre-analytic affective signal of concern or urgency before formal reasoning
Probabilistic updating of hypothesis probability given new evidence
Retrieval and adaptation of an analogous prior clinical case
Speculative exploratory phase before any formal hypothesis
Translation of raw case data into a diagnostic semantic frame

Choice.hint: Personal narrative of an analogous patient encounter
Reasoning about absent or negating evidence
Application of a protocol, guideline, or algorithm
Reference to accumulated clinical experience (no specific story)
Test selection explicitly tied to hypothesis discrimination
Explicit reversal: 'however this would not explain...', 'upon reconsideration...'
Absent finding cited as evidence: 'notably absent was X', 'lack of X argues against Y'
Anchoring bias: sticking to initial hypothesis despite contradictory new evidence
Confirmatory bias: only seeking evidence supporting the current hypothesis
Availability bias: over-weighting a recently seen or memorable diagnosis
Representativeness bias: over-relying on prototype match, ignoring base rates
Premature closure: accepting a diagnosis without adequately excluding alternatives
Tentative flag for verifiably incorrect medical claim — requires expert review

TextArea.name: notes
TextArea.placeholder: Optional: explain boundary cases or uncertainty
